segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a black screen, which then transitions to a dark reddish-brown background displaying white Cyrillic text. As the production details for the Soviet animated film 'Снежная Королева' (The Snow Queen) begin to appear, a dramatic and sweeping orchestral score commences, featuring strings, brass, and percussion, setting a grand and slightly mysterious tone. The text first credits 'Союзмультфильм' (Soyuzmultfilm) studio in Moscow, 1957, followed by the film's title, set against a stylized icy backdrop with jagged ice formations, and acknowledging H.C. Andersen as the story's author. The formal, static presentation continues as the creative team is listed: screenwriters G. Grebner, L. Atamanov, and N. Erdman; poet N. Zabolotsky; and lyricist M. Svetlov. The orchestral music swells and evolves, providing a rich, cinematic accompaniment as the director L. Atamanov, art directors N. Fedorov and I. Sharzman, cinematographer M. Druian, and composer A. Aivazyan are credited. The score maintains its epic quality as sound engineer N. Prilutsky and editor L. Kyakht are shown, followed by a comprehensive list of animators including F. Khitruk, E. Khudova, K. Chikin, R. Mirenkova, T. Fedorova, V. Krumin, L. Rezцova, V. Likhachev, I. Podgorsky, V. Dolgikh, E. Komova, and G. Novozhilov. Assistant animators B. Korneev, L. Model, and A. Dudnikov, assistant directors V. Shilina, L. Goryacheva, and N. Orlova, assistant cameramen N. Klimova and E. Rizo, and background artists I. Svetlich, O. Gimmerling, P. Korobayev, and D. Anpilov are also credited. The film’s director R. Ivanov is then credited, and finally, the voice actors are listed with their roles: V. Gribov as Ole-Lukoye, Y. Zheimo as Gerda, A. Komolova as Kay, M. Babanova as the Snow Queen, and G. Kozhakina as the Little Robber Girl. Throughout these sequential credits, the background remains consistently dark and icy, evoking a classic Soviet animation aesthetic, all underscored by the continuous, powerful orchestral music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an orchestral, slightly mysterious and whimsical score playing over a static title card, crediting voice actors like В. Грибков, Я. Жеймо, А. Комолова, М. Бабанова, and Г. Кожакина in white Cyrillic text against a dark, cracked ice background. The scene then transitions to a close-up of a large, ornate book titled 'Сказки и истории' by Г. Х. Андерсен. A small animated gnome-like character, with a red hat, white hair, red coat, and green tunic, appears atop the book. As the gnome begins to climb down the side of the book, making grunting and straining sounds, a male voice exclaims, "Погодите, погодите!" The gnome then picks up a small white umbrella with a blue handle, and as it walks along the book’s spine with a determined expression, the voice announces, "Первый зонтик!" and later, "Второй!" accompanied by more grunts. A sudden "Ой!" from the gnome is followed by a magical whoosh and chime sound effect. After a brief "Да," and some chuckles, the gnome, now standing upright and holding the umbrella as if ready for an adventure, addresses the viewer with a friendly, "А теперь здравствуйте. Я очень рад вас видеть." The dim lighting throughout emphasizes the book and the character, maintaining a whimsical atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dimly lit, shadowy study or library, imbued with a somber, moody atmosphere, as mysterious, whimsical music with a magical twinkle begins to play. A small gnome-like character, the Гном-персонаж, with a red hat, white hair, red coat, and green tunic, climbs down from a dark bookshelf. He picks up a small white umbrella with a blue handle and walks along the book’s spine with a determined expression. A theatrical male voice, belonging to the gnome, begins to speak in Russian, addressing "Hans Christian Andersen, friend of children all over the world." The camera pans to reveal a dark statue of a man in formal attire, while the gnome's voice continues, stating that "birds, flowers, chairs, and even this old cabinet" (referencing the room's scholarly furniture including a grandfather clock, wooden table with candelabra, and open book) all told Andersen their stories. The gnome then sits atop the statue’s pedestal, smiling and gesturing cheerfully, as he reveals that Andersen learned many stories from him, and even said, "No one in the world knows as many fairy tales as Ole Lukøje." Exclaiming "Yes!", the gnome stands, strikes a confident pose, raising his fist in triumph, then removes his red hat to reveal his bald head with white sideburns. He continues to gesture joyfully with open arms, introducing himself as "Ole Lukøje," and with a mischievous chuckle, adds that ancient people called him the "God of Dreams," before humbly concluding, "I'm just a little magical man." The low, dramatic lighting enhances the mysterious and whimsical tone throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a small, gnome-like character, the Гном-персонаж, with a red hat and coat, initially distressed and clutching his head on a dark, shadowy surface. As soft, magical background music begins, a calm male voice narrates in Russian, describing how "in the evenings, when you lay your head on the pillow and cover yourselves with a blanket," he "tiptoes up to each of you and quietly blows on the back of your head," accompanied by a gentle blowing sound. The gnome then shifts to an expression of surprise or realization, raising his hands. The narrator continues, "I'll blow, your little eyes close, and you... fall asleep," with a soft, drawn-out "shhh" sound. The gnome picks up a closed umbrella with colorful heart and leaf patterns, which lies in the dimly lit room with a large dark vase. The narrator states, "Then I open the umbrella," as the gnome opens it with a flourish and begins to dance joyfully, twirling it around with a wide smile. The narrator specifies, "This colorful one, over good, sweet children," and adds, "I start to spin it, like this," as the gnome continues his expressive twirling. The gnome then closes the umbrella, places it down, and gestures with excitement, celebrating as the narrator concludes, "And they dream fascinating fairy tales all night long." The low lighting and sharp shadows emphasize the gnome's internal emotional journey, which visually mirrors the soothing, dream-inducing narrative.

He clutches a black umbrella, his expression shifting from worry to joy as he begins to dance and twirl the umbrella with enthusiasm. He continues, "But I have no doubt, you are all very nice, sweet, obedient, not bullies, not lazybones! And therefore I will tell you a wonderful fairy tale. Listen and watch!" The scene briefly cuts to black before returning to the gnome, now standing beside a large dark vase adorned with a feather, with a closed patterned umbrella leaning against it. He gestures excitedly, then opens the Зонт с узором сердечек и листьев, revealing its white canopy with colorful heart and leaf patterns. He twirls it joyfully, his movements expressive and lively. As the camera zooms into the umbrella’s pattern, which transitions into a surreal, animated view of a miniature town with red-roofed houses, cobblestone streets, and intricate architectural details, the gnome declares, "Before you is an ancient city." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dramatic, melancholic orchestral score as a shadowy view from beneath a large, ornate umbrella reveals a miniature town with red-roofed buildings and cobblestone streets, suggesting a magical transformation. The music then shifts to a whimsical, playful tune as the camera pans across the intricate architecture and warm, golden lighting. A man in a black coat and top hat walks briskly through the streets, holding a closed umbrella with a serious expression. He passes a plump woman in a blue dress and red apron, who is startled by his presence and stumbles backward, exclaiming in Russian, 'Ой, что я наделала!' The scene shifts to a sunlit balcony where two young girls with blonde hair are tending to flower boxes. Amidst bird chirping, a black-and-white bird flies into the frame and lands on the balcony, causing the girls to react with surprise and delight, exclaiming 'Смотри! Смотри!' (Look! Look!) and giggling happily. The bird then flies away, and the girls resume their gardening, their happy chatter and giggles filling the air, transitioning the overall atmosphere from mysterious and shadowy to bright and cheerful, emphasizing a shift from solitude to community and warmth.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens on a rustic wooden roof corner with a small horn-like adornment, before panning down to a sunlit balcony. Here, a young girl, Gerda, with long blonde hair in a blue blouse and red skirt, is watering plants. She speaks in Russian, saying, "The swallow brought a flower. I also want to make a gift for myself. Here. I grew it for you." Another girl, Kai, with short blonde hair in a dark brown outfit, approaches, carrying a potted red rose. Kai responds, "And this one I grew for you, Gerda." Gerda thanks her, then suggests, "Kai, let's replant them in one pot." Kai agrees, and with shared laughter, they kneel together, gently placing the red rose beside a white one. Gerda expresses their wish for the flowers to be "inseparable, like us," and "friends, like us," and "love each other." Kai echoes, "And let them love each other, like us," to which Gerda affirms. A man's voice calls out "Kai! Gerda!" from off-screen. The girls then stand, and Gerda closes the balcony door. As the camera zooms out to reveal the quaint town with red-tiled roofs and distant church spires, Gerda says, "Goodbye, Gerda," and Kai says, "Goodbye, Kai." The peaceful atmosphere of the miniature village is underscored by the soft, warm lighting and the gentle, whimsical orchestral music that begins to play.

She is watering the plants with a small metal watering can. A second girl, with short blonde hair and dressed in a dark brown outfit, approaches carrying a potted red rose. The two girls kneel together, placing the red rose beside the white one in a shared pot, and they gently touch the flowers, smiling at each other in a moment of quiet companionship. The camera pulls back to show the full balcony, framed by ivy and overlooking a quaint town with red-tiled roofs and distant church spires. The girls then stand up, and the first girl closes the balcony door, turning away as the camera zooms out to reveal the broader townscape, emphasizing the cozy, peaceful atmosphere of the miniature village. The lighting is soft and warm, evoking a serene, pastoral mood.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a sweeping view of a quaint, medieval-style town, its red-tiled roofs and timber-framed houses transitioning from a bright, clear sky to gathering dark clouds, then rain, and finally a heavy snowfall, blanketing the streets in white. Throughout this visual transformation, a gentle, slightly melancholic background music plays, as a male narrator's voice in Russian sets the scene, speaking of deep love, summers spent in a garden, and the onset of cold, rainy, and long winter evenings, during which 'Kai' would visit and 'her grandmother' would tell stories to the children. The scene then shifts indoors to a cozy, warmly lit room where the elderly woman, now clearly identified as the grandmother, with white hair, wearing a red hooded cloak and spectacles, sits knitting in a wooden chair. Beside her, two young blonde-haired girls—one in a blue top and red skirt, the other in a dark brown outfit—sit quietly, observing. The room is adorned with a decorative fireplace, from which the distinct sound of crackling fire and bubbling water emanates, and a window with snow-covered panes, through which a potted plant with a pink and red rose is visible. The grandmother's calm, warm voice is heard, asking the children if they listen well when firewood crackles and it's frosty outside. She then points out, 'Look, it's snowing again, and every snowflake is like a white bee.' One of the girls, with an excited, high-pitched young voice, then asks, 'Grandma, tell me, where do these white bee-snowflakes fly from?' to which the grandmother replies, 'From the far north, from there...' The camera emphasizes the contrast between the cold, snowy exterior and the warm, intimate interior, as the girls remain absorbed in the moment, listening to the stories and the rhythmic click of knitting needles.

Suddenly, a violent burst of snow and ice erupts from the left, sweeping across the frame and transforming the cozy interior into a cold, icy landscape. The narrator then reveals that "all of them are loyal servants of the Snow Queen." Dramatic, ominous orchestral music swells as the camera follows the snowstorm, revealing a towering, crystalline Ледяной замок emerging from the darkness, illuminated by glowing embers and falling snowflakes. A deep, resonant gong sounds, followed by an eerie, sustained hum, as the narrator confirms, "There stands an ice palace." The scene shifts to the interior of this castle, where the Снежная Королева, a regal, pale figure with a sharp, angular headdress and icy blue eyes, is seated on her dark throne. The narrator states, "In this palace lives the Snow Queen herself." She rises and walks through the cavernous, jagged ice halls, her presence commanding and cold. Gentle, twinkling music begins as the narrator describes her, "She is beautiful. It's hard to imagine how good she is." The narrator continues, detailing that she is "all made of dazzling and sparkling ice," and "yet alive," with eyes that "shine like stars, but have no warmth," emphasizing the stark, magical chill of her domain.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a regal, pale figure, the Снежная Королева, seated on a dark, imposing throne within a cavernous, icy hall, exuding cold authority. She wears a sharp, angular headdress and holds a staff, her icy blue eyes fixed forward as she turns her head slightly, suggesting awareness or contemplation. A female narrator's voice, accompanied by grand, slightly mysterious orchestral music, describes the "many wonders in her cold palace," including an "ice mirror" through which the Snow Queen "sees all her possessions." The music shifts to an ominous, low drone as the scene abruptly transitions to a swirling vortex of darkness, then to a vast, desolate icy landscape under a black sky, where a lone reindeer trots across the snow. The narrator continues, detailing "boundless snowy fields" and "seas bound by ice," as the camera pans across jagged ice formations and icicles. The music becomes more intense and suspenseful as the narrator explains that the Snow Queen "often at night flies through city streets and looks into windows." The visual then shifts to a cozy, snow-covered cottage with glowing orange windows, where snowflakes gently fall. Inside, a warm, dimly lit room reveals a Старушка knitting in a wooden chair, flanked by two young Девочки на балконе, one in a blue top and red skirt, the other in a dark brown outfit. The suspenseful music continues to build. Suddenly, one of the girls gasps and exclaims, "Вот она!" (There she is!) as the girl in red points toward the window. The camera focuses on a framed portrait of the Снежная Королева’s face, which appears to be watching them. The other girl adds, "Смотрит на нас." (She's looking at us.) The girls react with surprise and concern, the girl in red covering her mouth, while the other looks on with wide eyes. The Старушка calmly reassures them, asking, "Чего ты боишься? Тут никого нет." (What are you afraid of? There's no one here), then playfully calls one a "Трусишка." (Scaredy-cat.) The girls, still wide-eyed, whisper, "Ах... она... Снежная..." (Oh... she... Snow...) with the other finishing, "Королева." (Queen.) The atmosphere shifts from the cold, magical realm of the Snow Queen to the warmth and innocence of the cottage, creating a stark contrast between the external icy world and the internal safety of the home, underscored by the dialogue and music.

I'll put her on a hot stove, and she'll melt!' followed by his own laughter. Suddenly, with a sound of shattering ice and a whoosh of wind, the perspective shifts as a large, translucent, icy figure—Снежная Королева—emerges from a shattered ice formation, her pale, angular form glowing with cold light. She raises a staff, and with a powerful, commanding voice, begins a magical incantation: 'I conjure you, icy shards! I conjure you, with my magical power! Fly forth, and in the wild roar of the wind, rush over the earth, and pierce the eyes and hearts of these foolish people! And he, into whose eye a shard pierces, let him see only evil everywhere, and he, into whose heart glass pierces, let him...' As she speaks, accompanied by eerie, magical background music and the sound of rushing wind, she unleashes a powerful, swirling vortex of ice and snow that engulfs the environment. The camera follows the chaotic spiral as it tears through a dark, wintry landscape, eventually revealing the towering, crystalline structure of the Ледяной замок, illuminated by a bright, ethereal light. The atmosphere shifts from the warmth of the interior to the harsh, magical cold of the Snow Queen’s domain, emphasizing her dominion over ice and winter.

A distinct, slightly raspy voice, likely the Gnome-персонаж, cackles, "Выбросьте их на помойку!" followed by more laughter, aligning with the gnome's transition from crying to joy. Gerda, still distressed, calls out, "Бабушка! Бабушка!" The elderly woman, the Старушка, seated calmly knitting in a red hooded cloak, responds gently, "Не плачь, Герда. Не плачь." As Gerda sobs, the Старушка adds, "Бедняжка Кай. Его сердце превратилось в кусочек льда. Он стал..." her voice trailing off as melancholic music underscores her words. He teases her, warning, "You'll fall, you'll cry," but she retorts, "You used to pull me before, Kai. And I didn't fall. And if I fall, I won't cry." After she playfully throws the apple, which bounces off a snow-covered wall, the boy chases it. The girl then trips and falls onto a small wooden sled, prompting Kai to playfully remark, "Well, sit down! There, you didn't fall! There, you won't cry!" as he helps her up. She defiantly declares, "Look, Kai! I'm not crying! Truly, I'm not crying!" as she brushes snow off her dress. As they resume walking, an upbeat, whimsical orchestral score begins, swelling with a lively, celebratory feel. The camera pulls back to reveal a wider view of the cheerful, snow-covered town, where numerous children, including the girl calling out "Kai! Come to us! Kai, come!", are joyfully playing in the snow, sledding, running, and laughing, their excited shouts blending with the vibrant music. The soft, diffused lighting highlights the colorful winter clothing against the muted stone architecture, emphasizing the warmth of their play amidst the cold, bright winter day, with snow-covered rooftops, stone buildings, and a church steeple in the background.

A figure in a blue coat rides in the sleigh, appearing to be in pursuit or escape. As the sleigh races past snow-laden houses, the girl's distressed voice is heard crying out, "Куда же ты!" The camera follows the sleigh's swift movement as it exits the village and transitions to a darker, forested area under a night sky. The sleigh continues its journey through snow-covered trees, its galloping hooves and sleigh bells echoing through the tense atmosphere. The girl, now in a red dress with a blue collar and brown hood, is seen running frantically, her face showing distress, her cries still resonating. The sleigh disappears into the dense, shadowy woods, emphasizing the urgency and danger of the chase, all set against the backdrop of classic Soviet-era animation and the persistent, dramatic music.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dark, snowy forest at night, accompanied by dramatic, ominous orchestral music that sets a tense, magical atmosphere. A white sleigh, pulled by two powerfully galloping white horses, races urgently through the snow-covered terrain. The Снежная Королева, a tall, pale figure in a blue coat and white fur-trimmed cloak, drives the sleigh, pursuing a small boy, the Мальчик в коричневом пальто и белой шапке, who runs desperately through the snow. As the boy stumbles and falls, the Queen reaches down with a cold, emotionless gesture, lifting him into the sleigh. She speaks to him with a calm yet menacing tone, asking, "Так ты хотел посадить меня на горячую печку?" (So you wanted to put me on a hot stove?) and then, "Ну ты совсем замёрз?" (Well, are you completely frozen?). The boy, visibly frightened, clutches his hands together as he is held. The Queen continues, "Ну иди ко мне." (Well, come to me.) and "Всё ещё мёрзнешь?" (Still freezing?). The boy, distressed, cries out in a high-pitched voice, "Мне холодно! И больно! Отпусти!" (I'm cold! And it hurts! Let go!). The Queen responds chillingly, "Больше не буду тебя целовать." (I won't kiss you anymore.). The orchestral music intensifies dramatically as the sleigh continues its swift journey through the icy forest, passing snow-laden trees that glisten under the dark sky. The chase culminates as the sleigh speeds away into the darkness, leaving the boy trapped in the queen’s grasp, his fate uncertain, as the powerful music fades slightly but maintains its intensity.

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence opens with a dramatic nighttime chase through a dark, snowy forest, underscored by ominous, magical orchestral music, sleigh bells, and the thudding of galloping horses. The Снежная Королева, a tall, pale figure in a blue coat with white fur trim, rides swiftly in a white sleigh pulled by two galloping white horses over icy terrain. She captures the frightened Мальчик в коричневом пальто и белой шапке, holding him tightly as she speaks in a cold, echoing voice: "Доверься мне, мой мальчик. Мы отсюда умчимся в удивительное царство. Попав туда, ты обо всём забудешь. Холодной льдинкой сделается сердце. Не будет в нём ни радости, ни горя. Но лишь покой и холод. Это счастье." The sleigh then soars through a starry night sky, the boy looking up in awe and fear as the stars swirl around them, while the ethereal orchestral music continues. The scene transitions to a close-up of a large, ornate book titled 'Сказки и истории' by Г. Х. Андерсен, from which emerges the Гном-персонаж. The music shifts to a whimsical, almost circus-like tune as the small gnome-like figure, initially crying with a handkerchief, transitions to joyfully singing or speaking with expressive vocalizations, twirling a white umbrella with heart and leaf patterns. The final shot reveals a dimly lit room with a dark, life-sized statue of a man, a large dark vase, and a closed umbrella, as the music becomes more melancholic and a faint, almost whispered "Прошла" (It passed) is heard, marking a narrative transition.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a dimly lit room where a small, animated gnome-like character, with white hair, a red hat, red coat, and green tunic, initially appears distressed, wiping his eyes with a handkerchief. He then transitions to a joyful expression, gesturing animatedly as he narrates in a storytelling male voice, describing how "Winter came. But Kai did not return. Little Gerda waited for him in vain." He continues, explaining that "when spring finally came, Gerda put on her best scarves and new red shoes, kissed her sleeping grandmother, and set off in search of Kai," emphasizing her as "brave, little Gerda." The gnome stands beside a large dark vase with a feathered adornment and a closed umbrella with heart and leaf patterns, which remain static. The scene then transitions to a bright, open field where the young girl, Gerda, with blonde hair and wearing a red dress with a blue bodice and white sleeves, walks through a meadow of white flowers, accompanied by orchestral music that begins to swell. Birds fly overhead, and a distant city skyline is visible. Gerda pauses, looks around, and continues walking along a dirt path where she encounters a small, playful lamb. She is heard asking in a questioning voice, "Please tell me, do you know where my brother Kai is?" to which multiple high-pitched, bird-like voices respond, "We don't know, we don't know, we don't know, we don't know!" Gerda then asks the lamb, "Maybe you know where Kai is?" The lamb, in a high-pitched, squeaky voice, humorously replies, "I don't know! I was just born!" The lamb hops and runs ahead, leading Gerda toward a riverbank where a wooden boat rests under a weeping willow tree. Gerda approaches the boat, placing her hands on her chest in a gesture of wonder or emotion, and is heard addressing the river, "River... River, tell me, maybe... maybe Kai..." as the scene ends with a soft, pastoral atmosphere, gentle flowing water sounds, and the continuing orchestral music.

segments.integrated_caption: Under a soft, hazy sky, a serene riverbank with a weeping willow sets the scene as a small wooden boat rests on the shore. A girl, dressed in a red skirt, white sleeves, and dark blue vest, approaches with wonder. She picks up a red leather shoe, declaring in Russian, "Here are my new shoes. Take them, I don't mind. Just tell me where Kai is." As gentle, slightly melancholic orchestral music plays, she places the shoe into the boat, asking, "Don't you want my gift? It can't be that you don't know. Please, take it!" With deliberate motion, she unties the rope and steps into the boat, pushing off into the calm water. Her blonde hair flows as she rows gently, gliding down the river under the willow's branches, while birds, including swallows, fly overhead. The music becomes more hopeful and determined as she exclaims, "Thank you! Are you taking me to Kai?" The boat moves deeper into a lush, green riverside landscape, and the music grows dramatic and adventurous. The girl stands up, arms outstretched, as if embracing the natural beauty, speaking rapidly and excitedly, almost in a chant or plea, as the orchestral intensity builds.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a close-up of a wooden boat nestled under a weeping willow tree by a calm river, its surface reflecting the sky and clouds, as whimsical, brass-heavy orchestral music begins to play. A girl with long blonde hair, in a red skirt, white sleeves, and a dark blue vest, emerges from behind the foliage and approaches the boat with wonder. She places a red shoe inside, unties the rope, and gently rows the boat into the river, passing under the willow’s branches as birds fly overhead. The setting transitions to a sunlit riverside estate with a white stone wall, a wooden gate adorned with green ivy and white flowers, and two uniformed guards standing at attention, holding brass instruments. As the girl rows toward the shore, she exclaims "Солдатики!" (Soldiers!) and then "Замолчите!" (Be quiet!) in a high-pitched voice, seemingly reacting to the guards' brass music, which continues with a whimsical, almost toy-like quality. A tall, imposing woman in a black coat with a wide-brimmed hat, holding a long blue cane, waits. The guards salute as the girl disembarks. The woman, identified as the Старуха, gestures with her cane, then bends down to greet the girl warmly, placing a hand on her shoulder. In a gentle, slightly aged voice, the Старуха asks, "Ах ты бедная крошечка. Здравствуйте! Как ты попала на эту большую и быструю?" (Oh, you poor little crumb. Hello! How did you get on this big and fast [river/boat implied]?). The girl reaches out to touch the woman’s hand, sharing a moment of connection before the woman leads her through the gate into the estate, while the guards, detailed with white trousers, black boots, and tall hats with gold trim, remain motionless, and the orchestral music transitions to a more dramatic, melancholic tone.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens at a grand, ivy-covered gate, where two uniformed guards in white trousers, black boots, and tall black hats with gold trim stand at attention, each holding a brass instrument, as gentle, melancholic orchestral music plays. A tall, elderly woman in a black coat with a wide-brimmed hat and a long blue cane, the Старуха, greets a young girl with long blonde hair, the Девочка, who wears a red skirt, white sleeves, and a dark blue vest, arriving with a small white bag. The girl, in a young, determined voice, asks, “Я ищу моего названного братца Кая” (I'm looking for my adopted brother Kai). The Старуха warmly welcomes her, gesturing for her to enter, and with a kind voice, says, “Пойдём, пойдём. Ты голодна и устала” (Let's go, let's go. You are hungry and tired). As they walk through the gate into a lush garden filled with vibrant flowers and a trellis covered in roses, the girl exclaims, “Ах, какие красивые цветы!” (Oh, what beautiful flowers!) and then asks, “Можно, я спрошу у них, где Кай?” (Can I ask them where Kai is?). The Старуха leads her to a stone bench beneath the trellis, where she gently places the girl down and removes her hat, revealing a kind expression. With a soothing tone, the Старуха tells her, “Ты сама как цветок. И мне давно, давно хотелось иметь такую миленькую, маленькую девочку” (You yourself are like a flower. And I have long, long wanted to have such a sweet, little girl). The girl lies down on the bench, appearing tired or perhaps in need of rest, initially resisting by saying, “Нет, нет! Я должна искать Кая!” (No, no! I must look for Kai!) but the Старуха watches over her with care, gently urging, “Ложись и отдохни. Спи, спи. Забудь обо всём” (Lie down and rest. Sleep, sleep. Forget everything), as the serene atmosphere, enhanced by soft lighting, rich floral details, and bird chirping, suggests a safe haven and a transition from journey to rest.

Beside her stands a tall, elderly woman, identified by a male narrator as a sorceress, in a black coat and wide-brimmed hat, holding a long blue cane, gently placing her hand on Gerda’s shoulder in a protective or comforting gesture. The narrator explains that this old woman, though not evil, is now casting spells to keep Gerda with her at all costs, intending for Gerda to forget everything, especially Kai. The final shot reveals a magical, glowing garden of flowers with ethereal light emanating from their centers, suggesting a transition to a fantastical or enchanted realm, as the music continues with a determined yet slightly melancholic orchestral theme.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dark, moonlit garden, an ethereal atmosphere created by a white lattice gazebo and glowing flowers, all set to a melancholic, almost eerie orchestral score. A young girl with long blonde hair, wearing a red skirt, white sleeves, and a dark blue vest, lies asleep on a stone bench with a white pillow. An elderly woman, the 'Старуха в чёрном пальто и шляпе', gently places her hand on the girl’s shoulder, waking her. The girl sits up, looking confused and distressed, her voice filled with confusion as she asks, "Зачем я здесь? Куда шла? Зачем? Ничего не помню." The old woman helps her to her feet. The girl then walks through the garden, her expression shifting from drowsiness to sorrow, her voice now tinged with deep sadness, "Почему мне так тяжело? У меня какое-то горе." She stops before a bush with a red and a white rose, gently touching the white rose, which appears wilting or damaged. A gasp escapes her as she says, "Розы... Розы... белые... алые..." Her face contorts in grief as she clutches her chest, and with a sudden, sharp realization, she exclaims, "Вспомнила!" accompanied by a dramatic musical swell and a deep chime. She then turns away, her eyes filled with sadness, and walks toward a distant, illuminated house, her steps heavy with emotion, as the dark, shadowy environment with soft moonlight continues to emphasize a mood of melancholy and loss, underscored by the ongoing dramatic music.

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence begins with a soldier in a red coat, white cross-belts, and a tall black hat, standing in a dimly lit, rocky cavern, saluting as ethereal, slightly melancholic music starts. A girl, identified by her soft, pleading Russian voice as Gerda, with long blonde hair, wearing a red skirt, white sleeves, and a dark blue vest, appears beside him. She plays a golden horn with a focused expression, while her voice is heard saying, "Не надо... Не шумить... Не трубите... Пожалейте маленькую Герду." The background shifts to a floral, shadowy garden as the soldier remains silently behind her. Gerda approaches a large, arched stone door, continuing to speak, "Калитка... Милая калитка... Мне надо искать Кая. Выпусти меня, пожалуйста." She knocks and pushes the door open with a determined gesture, accompanied by the distinct sound of a large, heavy door creaking, and then says, "Спасибо." As she steps through, revealing a lush, flower-filled garden with a distant, fairytale-style house, a powerful, triumphant horn fanfare sounds, and the music swells. Gerda turns back briefly, then walks gracefully and purposefully into the garden, her voice becoming increasingly distressed and desperate as she repeatedly calls out "Кай!" and "Помоги! Кай!" The scene abruptly cuts to a dark, desolate landscape under a stormy sky with footprints, then to a dramatic seascape at dusk with waves crashing against jagged rocks and wind sounds, as the music becomes chaotic and then fades, evoking a sense of isolation and foreboding.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a desolate, stormy coastal landscape under a heavy, dark sky, with turbulent waves crashing against jagged rocks, accompanied by dramatic, ominous orchestral music. A flock of white birds flies erratically across the frame. The camera then focuses on a young girl with long blonde hair, wearing a blue vest over a white blouse and a red skirt, sitting pensively on a rock by the shore. She appears distressed, clutching her hands together, and softly mutters, "О, не плачь, Кая." A black-and-white bird, resembling a magpie or crow, approaches her from the right, landing near her feet and looking up with an inquisitive gaze. As the music shifts to a more whimsical, almost circus-like tune, a deep, theatrical male voice greets her with "Здравствуйте." The girl stands up, her expression shifting to cautious curiosity, and she replies, "Здравствуйте." The male voice introduces himself, "Меня зовут Каракс," to which the girl responds, "Очень приятно." Karaks then asks, "Почему вы гуляете так поздно и совсем одна? Неужели вам, [bird sound], не страшно?" The girl, now following the bird as it turns and walks away, her bare feet touching the dark, wet sand, admits, "Мне очень страшно, но я ищу Кая." She then addresses him directly, "Постойте, Каракс. Может быть, вы знаете, где..." The atmosphere remains somber and foreboding, with the dark clouds and churning sea emphasizing a sense of isolation and impending danger, as the girl follows the bird, seeking help in her quest.

segments.integrated_caption: Under a stormy, dark sky, a black-and-white bird, resembling a magpie or crow, stands inquisitively on a desolate, rocky shore. An orchestral score begins to swell as a male voice calls out "Кай!" twice, then "Привет, мой мальчик!" The scene cuts to a distressed girl, barefoot in a red skirt and blue vest, on the same bleak shore. A gentle female voice, likely hers, describes someone as "хороший, добрый, смелый" (good, kind, brave), echoed by the male voice. As the girl hesitantly walks, the bird follows closely. The male voice narrates, "Недавно у нас появился один такой" (Recently, one like that appeared to us), prompting the girl's hopeful query, "Может быть, это Кай?" (Maybe it's Kai?). The male voice confirms his appearance and states, "И очень понравился нашей принцессе" (And our princess liked him very much), to which the girl's voice responds, "Кай не может не нравиться" (Kai cannot but be liked). As Kai examines the crystal, the Queen's calm, authoritative voice is heard, remarking on the crystal's "precision" and acknowledging Kai's intelligence, calling him by name: "Ты стал очень умным, Кай." Kai softly responds "Да," then observes, "Но они совсем не пахнут." The Queen slowly rises from her throne, her hands resting on its armrests, as ominous, magical background music begins to play. She continues to lecture Kai, stating, "Кай, я много раз говорила тебе: аромат цветов, красота и радость, стихи поэтов и любовь... Их не существует." The camera alternates between Kai's awestruck face, which shifts to a mix of fear and fascination as he looks up, and the Queen's imposing figure. She extends her hand slightly, a subtle gesture of command, and forcefully instructs him, "Ты должен забыть все это, Кай. Забыть!" Kai, still kneeling and clutching the crystal, responds with a soft, resigned "Да," then repeats, "Забыть." The scene emphasizes the power dynamic, with the Queen's presence dominating the dim, blue-toned icy hall. The background music swells with a melancholic intensity as the Queen then asks, "Ты знаешь, что такое радость, Кай?" Kai's young, subdued voice replies, "Нет." She presses further, "А красота?" to which he responds, "Нет, не знаю," reinforcing the cold, oppressive atmosphere and his indoctrination. The sequence ends with Kai still in awe, the crystal glowing faintly, as the Queen watches silently, her expression unreadable but authoritative.

Eerie, high-pitched strings create a chilling atmosphere as she slowly rises, extending her hand. Her cold, ethereal voice, with a slight echo, asks in Russian, "Ты помнишь, что такое любовь, Кай? Любовь?" The camera pulls back to reveal the vast ice formations, and in the foreground, the Мальчик в коричневом пальто и белой шапке, implied to be Kai, kneels, clutching a glowing blue crystal. His expression shifts from curiosity to awe and slight fear as he softly and hesitantly replies in Russian, "Не знаю." The Queen continues, "Я помню Герду. Герда? Да. Ты помнишь Герду? Да. Ничего. Ты забудешь и это. Скоро твое сердце станет совсем ледяным," her voice prophesying his icy fate. The chilling music intensifies before transitioning to an upbeat, fanfare-like orchestral piece as the scene cuts to a warm, sunlit courtyard. Here, a golden Карета с лошадьми, drawn by two black horses, arrives at a grand estate, accompanied by the distinct sounds of horse hooves and carriage wheels. A theatrical male voice announces in Russian, "Герда! Мы дарим тебе эту золотую карету. И ещё и лошадочку." A Женщина в синем платье и красном фартуке waves from a balcony, while chefs unload food. The stark contrast between the cold, magical realm and the lively domestic scene, underscored by the celebratory music, suggests a narrative shift or flashback. The carriage moves away, and the scene fades to black as the music also fades.

The gnome dashes toward ornate steps where a plump woman in a blue and brown gown with a high, feathered headdress stands, gesturing imperiously, her stern adult female voice wishing, "Желаю тебе скорее найти Кая!" Two black crows, previously perched on the steps, are startled and take flight with distinct "Карр, карр, карр!" caws as a young boy, identified as Gerda, in a white hooded cloak and red tunic, runs past, followed by a man in a blue tunic and yellow belt who appears to be chasing him. Gerda is then seen inside the carriage, looking out the window with a worried expression, his child-like female voice saying "Не ходить пешком," then "Хорошо, не буду," and later expressing "Спасибо! Спасибо!" and "До свидания! До свидания!" as the carriage departs with the sounds of horse hooves and carriage wheels. A deep, commanding adult male voice, likely the driver, wishes "Добрый путь, Герда!" The scene shifts to Gerda standing alone on a balcony, looking out at a distant landscape, before the carriage is shown moving along a tree-lined path, Gerda's voice reflecting, "Как добрые люди и животные." The whimsical music transitions to a more dramatic, slightly ominous orchestral piece as the carriage and its horses gallop through a dark, shadowy forest, silhouetted against a moonlit sky, suggesting a perilous journey ahead and emphasizing Gerda’s isolation and the urgency of her escape.

The scene ends with the attackers celebrating their loot, dancing around the pile of gold, accompanied by guttural laughter and triumphant shouts, as the carriage lies broken and abandoned in the dark forest, with the music shifting to a mischievous, triumphant tone.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit, cavernous forest setting with gnarled, icy trees, two bandits in black hats and red-trimmed clothing are seen looting a large pile of gold coins spilled from an overturned carriage, the coins clinking softly. One bandit, shorter and stockier with a wide-brimmed hat and a red sash, frantically counts and grabs coins, muttering in a deep, guttural voice, "Олопал, Миха." The other, taller and slimmer with a long black coat and a knife at his hip, watches with a smug expression. Suddenly, a small girl in a red hooded cloak with white fur trim and a white scarf appears, standing calmly amidst the chaos. The second bandit, with a high-pitched, almost childlike voice, exclaims "Девчонка!" and then laughs maniacally, describing her as "славненькая, жирненькая," suggesting, "Попробуем, какая ты на вкус." The first bandit, startled, drops his coins, gestures wildly, and attempts to hide his loot. The girl remains composed, observing them. As the first bandit tries to intimidate her by brandishing a knife, she calmly steps forward. The second bandit intervenes, grabbing her arm, prompting the girl to resist with high-pitched squeals and pull free, confronting him with a determined look, holding a small knife of her own. The first bandit, agitated, growls, "Ты что, кусаться? Ах ты дрянь!" The girl, with a clear, firm voice, commands, "Прочь! Это моя игрушка!" The second bandit, intrigued, remarks, "Ух, какая муфточка!" The first bandit gruffly demands, "Дай сюда!", while the second taunts, "На маму!" The girl again asserts herself, "Отойдите вы все! Ну, ну, ну!", with a small "Ой!" before the first bandit ironically tells her, "Не бойся." The scene is tense, with the bandits’ greed and aggression contrasting sharply with the girl’s quiet resolve, all set against the cold, shadowy environment where the gold coins gleam faintly under the dim light, accompanied by an eerie, whimsical background music.

The horse whinnies and struggles, rearing and causing Gerda to stumble and fall. Distressed, Gerda cries out, “Don't torment him!” The elderly woman dismisses this as “Nonsense” and “Calf tenderness.” As a white bird with outstretched wings flies swiftly past, the woman helps Gerda up, then addresses her, saying, “And now, now you too will live in my menagerie, princess.” Gerda, visibly exhausted and frustrated, sits on a stone ledge, refuting, “I am not a princess. I am just Gerda.” She explains, “I am walking across the wide world, looking for my foster brother Kai.” The elderly woman calls this “Stupid,” asks where he is, and upon Gerda's “I don't know,” exclaims “Terrible!” The claustrophobic and tense mood of the chamber is palpable throughout their interaction.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens under a moonlit sky with a wide, dark, snowy landscape, accompanied by dark, ominous, low string and brass music that soon transitions to a more melodic, melancholic orchestral theme. A young girl, the Ребёнок на олене, in a red coat with a white fur-trimmed hood, clings tightly to a large black reindeer with a white blaze and prominent antlers. The reindeer leaps dramatically across a snow-covered chasm, its powerful legs extended mid-air, as the girl holds on with determination and fear, while the music builds in tension. The camera follows their perilous journey across jagged ice and snowdrifts, the reindeer bounding over rocks and through sparse, snow-laden pine trees, the atmosphere tense and cold, emphasized by the dark, muted palette. A sudden sharp sound punctuates the urgent, faster-paced orchestral music as the scene transitions to a dimly lit cavern. Here, the reindeer stands near a roaring fire, and the Старушка, an elderly woman with long white hair and a red fur-trimmed coat, gestures anxiously, urging "Скорей! Милый! Скорей!" (Faster! Darling! Faster!) as the girl dismounts. The reindeer emits a distinct snort or grunt, prompting the Старушка to plead, "Ну, миленький, пожалуйста!" (Oh, darling, please!). The cavern, filled with hanging garments, offers warmth from the fire, contrasting sharply with the frigid exterior. The reindeer lowers its head, appearing calm, while the Старушка attempts to soothe it, her hands raised in reassurance. The scene ends with the reindeer standing still, firelight flickering on the stone walls, and the girl beside the Старушка, both looking toward the deer with concern, as the music continues its dramatic, active orchestral theme, and the Старушка sympathetically remarks, "Ах, вы бедняги, бедняги." (Oh, you poor things, poor things.)

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens inside a dimly lit, snowy cavern where a young girl in a red coat with white fur trim and a white hooded garment sits beside a boy dressed in orange and brown winter attire, near a small fire. An older female voice narrates in Russian, stating, "Правда, Снежной Королевы побывала здесь, но это было давно. Она помчалась дальше, в Финляндию." (Indeed, the Snow Queen was here, but that was long ago. She rushed further, to Finland.) The girl listens intently, then asks with a young voice, "А мой братец?" (And my brother?), to which the older voice responds, "Да-да, с ней был мальчик. Он сильно замёрз." (Yes, yes, there was a boy with her. He was very cold.) The girl then urgently exclaims, "Кай! Нам нужно скорее найти Кай, скорее!" (Kai! We need to find Kai quickly, quickly!), before the older voice laments, "Эх, жаль нет магии, нет бумаги. Ну, ладно." (Oh, it's a pity there's no magic, no paper. Well, alright.) As the boy picks up a large blue fish, the girl suddenly stands and rushes toward a large reindeer with a white chest and dark antlers, which is tethered nearby. The older voice instructs, "Так. Вот так. Отдашь старой финке. Она поможет тебе. Спеши! Спеши!" (So. Like this. You'll give it to the old Finn woman. She will help you. Hurry! Hurry!). The girl mounts the reindeer, and as the boy watches and then releases the reins, the reindeer gallops out of the cave into a vast, snow-covered landscape under a dusky sky. Dramatic, adventurous orchestral music swells, accompanied by the rhythmic sounds of galloping as the girl clings tightly to the reindeer’s neck, leaping across icy terrain and galloping past a herd of other reindeer. The camera follows their urgent escape across the frozen expanse, with distant mountains and dark forests in the background, emphasizing the adventure and peril of their journey.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment begins with a close-up of two children, a boy in a dark jacket and beige pants and a girl in a blue top and red skirt, sitting calmly on a snowy balcony surrounded by potted plants, as gentle, melancholic string music starts to play. The scene then transitions to a close-up of a white rose growing on a red-tiled roof, which dramatically opens to reveal a tiny gnome-like character inside. This gnome, with white hair, wearing a red coat, brown pants, and a red hat, holds a blue-handled sword and appears cheerful, gesturing with open arms. As the gnome gestures, a soft, elderly male voice, identified as the gnome's, reflects in Russian, saying, "Наши розы... Они снова расцвели." (Our roses... They bloomed again.) The music becomes more whimsical and magical as the gnome continues, "Смею думать, что у них и у вас впереди ещё много чудесных сказок. А этой сад, понимаете..." (I dare to think that they and you still have many wonderful fairy tales ahead. And this garden, you understand...). The music swells into a grand, orchestral sound, accompanying the fade to a dark, cloudy sky with a city silhouette and the light blue Cyrillic text 'КОНЕЦ СКАЗКИ' (End of the Tale). The segment concludes with production credits on a dark, textured background framed by ornate, icy borders, featuring the studio 'Союзмультфильм' (1948), the title 'Новогодняя ночь', and the creative team, all displayed in stylized white Cyrillic text with a frosty, crystalline design, as the music transitions to a traditional, intricate melody with chimes and strings, slowly fading out.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens inside a vast, crystalline ice cavern, part of the Ледяной замок, with jagged blue ice formations and glowing star-shaped crystals, all set to an upbeat, slightly frantic orchestral score. A small, red-robed Гном-персонаж, with white hair and a red hat, is seen struggling to pull a large, frost-covered Механический ящик through the icy terrain, accompanied by a small white rabbit. As he stops, places the box on a pedestal, and connects a microphone, distinct mechanical whirring, clanking, and ticking sounds emanate from the device. The gnome, identified as Дедушка Мороз, speaks into the microphone with a deep male voice, initially stating "Yes, New Year!" and reassuringly addressing someone as "Grandson, great! Don't worry, everything is ready." His tone quickly shifts to urgency and frustration, asking "What? Be quiet, you little ones! Are you joking or are you serious?" The camera then cuts to a close-up of a young boy, wearing a red and white winter outfit, black cap, and blue jacket, piloting a red and black Ракета-транспорт through a starry night sky. He grips the steering wheel tightly, his expression shifting from focused to alarmed as red sparks fly past the cockpit, and he responds with a high-pitched, urgent voice, frantically demanding, "Let's find them! Grandfather Frost! There must always be golden cones! You old man, hurry up! So that the golden ones are definitely there! So that the little ones shine!" The scene vividly contrasts the icy environment with the boy’s rocket, highlighting a frantic communication and escape attempt under pressure.

The rabbit, with a higher-pitched, helpful voice, advises him to 'ask the moon' and to let the 'little things glow.' The elder thanks the 'little bunny' and asks it to 'give me your paw.' He continues outside into a snowy forest at night, where he is joined by the Белый кролик and a Серый кролик, who follow him closely. The elder then leaps onto a snow-covered ledge, and the scene shifts to a wide shot of him standing on a snowy cliff under a crescent moon, looking out over the dark, starry sky, addressing the moon directly, 'Moon, crescent moon, show me the paths and roads. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a Santa-like figure, dressed in a red robe with white fur trim and a red hat, standing on a cloud in a starry night sky. He gestures toward a mischievous crescent moon, which playfully sprinkles snowflakes from its mouth, reacting with surprise and amusement. During this, the Santa-like figure, speaking in a deep, warm male voice, asks, "Может, знаешь понаслышке, где найти лесное чудо, ёлку, золотые шишки?" (Maybe you know by hearsay, where to find the forest miracle, a fir tree, golden cones?) and then advises, "Вон идёт медвежий след. По нему и топай, дед. Не сходи со следа зверьева, и упрёшься прямо в дерево." (There goes a bear's track. Follow it, old man. Don't stray from the animal's track, and you'll run straight into a tree.) He then turns and walks away as the moon continues to shower snow. The scene transitions to a red and white rocket flying erratically through the starry sky, emitting sparks and showing signs of distress or damage. A man in a black coat and top hat pilots the rocket, waving a flag. A different male voice, possibly the pilot, briefly thanks, "Ну, спасибо за указку, за внимание и за ласку." (Well, thank you for the pointer, for the attention and for the kindness.) The Santa-like figure then responds, "Рад уважить, река. Берегись лесовика!" (Glad to oblige, river. Beware of the forest spirit!). Dramatic music begins as a female voice, seemingly from the rocket, sings about a "steel bird" flying under "starry heavens" and needing to "appear at twelve o'clock" for the New Year, pushing its "engines at full throttle." The scene then shifts to a snowy forest where the Santa-like figure, now on the ground, interacts with two rabbits—a white one holding a glowing object and a grey one following closely. The Santa-like figure gestures toward them, and the white rabbit responds animatedly, creating a moment of gentle interaction in the cold, wintry environment, as the Santa-like figure comments, "Вот уж действительно красавица!" (Now that's truly a beauty!), and the female voice from the rocket briefly says, "Мне по..." (To me...).

segments.integrated_caption: A Santa-like figure, dressed in a traditional red robe with white fur trim, a red hat, and a long white beard, runs through a snowy, moonlit forest, carrying a large dark sack over his shoulder. He appears in a hurry, gesturing with his hands as if calling out or warning. He is accompanied by two rabbits, a white one and a grey one, who follow closely, occasionally looking up at him. A child-like female voice, belonging to one of the rabbits, asks in Russian, “Why don't you like it here?”, to which the Santa-like figure, in a deep, booming voice, reassures them, “Don't be afraid, bunny souls! Maybe we won't get lost!” and questions, “Whose jokes are these? I know whose!”. The environment shifts from dense, snow-covered trees to a more open, icy landscape with jagged ice formations and a glowing, crystalline tree, all set to an orchestral, slightly ominous and magical background score. The rabbit's voice then playfully accuses him, “Oh, I don't believe you, you're a coward!”, prompting the Santa-like figure to retort, “And you are terribly brave! Not a coward, you think, but you yourself are white with fear!”. He stops briefly to interact with the rabbits, who respond with animated gestures, the white rabbit even holding a small glowing object. The Santa-like figure continues his journey, climbing a snow-covered hill under a starry sky, his booming voice declaring, “Here you go! Here you go! With me, he'll cope with the frost!” before adding, “Oh, go to hell!”. He pauses at the summit to look around with a concerned expression, as if searching for something or someone, as the magical music continues with a sense of urgency. The atmosphere is cold and magical, with soft snowfall and a deep blue, nocturnal palette dominating the scene, ending with the figure standing atop the hill, gazing into the distance, his posture suggesting urgency and determination.

segments.integrated_caption: Under a dark, starry, snowy forest sky, a tall, elderly figure, identified as Ded Moroz, dressed in a red robe with white fur trim and a long white beard, stumbles and falls while carrying a large dark sack. He quickly recovers, brushing snow off himself, and continues running through the snow-covered terrain with urgency, accompanied by a tense, dramatic background score. He encounters a dark, bearded bandit in a brown tunic and orange scarf, who stealthily approaches from behind a tree with a mischievous grin. The bandit suddenly emerges, exclaiming in a taunting voice, "Теперь моя, красавица!" (Now you're mine, beauty!), and grabs a small white rabbit perched on a snow-covered stump, startling the vulnerable creature. Ded Moroz stops, startled, and gestures in alarm. The bandit then challenges Ded Moroz, asking, "Скажи, седая борода, зачем пожаловал сюда?" (Tell me, grey beard, why have you come here?), and declares, "Шутить с тобой мне недосужно. Знаешь сам, чего мне нужно." (I have no time for jokes with you. You know yourself what I need.) Ded Moroz, with an exasperated but firm tone, responds, "Да уж, конечно, знаю сам. Отдай же, дутки!" (Oh, of course, I know myself. Give it back, you rascal!), to which the bandit defiantly replies, "Дай! Не дам!" (Give it! I won't give it!). Ded Moroz calls him "Ты леший, всё-таки чудок." (You're a forest spirit, still a bit strange.) The bandit then turns to a large black bear, whom he calls "Мишка" (Mishka), and hands it a small wooden crate, urging, "А ну-ка, Мишка, вывалакивай из-под коряги ларчик лаковый!" (Hey, Mishka, drag out the lacquered casket from under the snag!). The bear sniffs the crate, then takes it with a growl, appearing aggressive. The bandit raises his arms triumphantly, challenging Ded Moroz, "Давай, старик, поборемся! Давай, старик, поспоримся! Что чудо почудеснее, что диво интереснее!" (Come on, old man, let's wrestle! Come on, old man, let's argue! What's more wondrous, what's more interesting!), signaling his success amidst the cold, foreboding backdrop of snow-covered trees and icy rocks, emphasizing the urgency and danger of the encounter.

segments.integrated_caption: In a snowy, starlit forest at night, a Santa-like figure in a red robe with white fur trim and a long white beard stumbles through the snow, brushing off snow from his hat and coat, accompanied by a small grey rabbit. A whimsical, slightly ominous orchestral score with Russian folk elements plays throughout. A bandit in a brown tunic and orange scarf, with a mischievous grin, approaches, holding a small, ornate mechanical box with a glowing screen. The bandit, in a higher-pitched, taunting voice, exclaims, "Glorious, it goes, bricks warm the back. Good. But not pretty. It will be prettier. And Petrov invented, a locomotive broom. Locomotive! Electric locomotives! That's what Santa Claus will say!" He hands the box to a large black bear, which sniffs it and then growls, taking it with a swipe of its paw. The Santa-like figure gestures urgently, pointing toward a distant, illuminated train whose whistle blows and chugging sounds are heard. The bandit then opens the box, revealing a circular, ornate plate with a painted scene that shifts from a castle on an island to a snowy landscape with a red-coated figure. The Santa-like figure reacts with alarm, and in a deeper, admonishing voice, warns, "Don't rejoice prematurely. The tree isn't yours yet. Look, Tsarevich Vanya is flying on a magic carpet." The bear, now holding the box, turns and runs off into the forest. The bandit, in his mischievous tone, dismisses, "Well, the carpet, at least it's not heavy. These are definitely miracles. It's a sin for me to argue with you. It just looks funny. This miracle is around, around like this!" The scene shifts to a close-up of the plate’s image, showing a red and black rocket-like vehicle flying over a snowy mountain range with a distinct whooshing sound. The Santa-like figure, now visibly distressed, raises his arms in frustration as the bear vanishes into the trees. The bandit, still holding the box, looks triumphant, then suddenly drops it as a white rabbit is snatched from a snow-covered stump by the bear. The Santa-like figure rushes forward, trying to intervene, but the bear flees with the rabbit and the box. The scene ends with the Santa-like figure standing alone in the snow, looking defeated, as the train continues its journey into the distance under a dark, starry sky, its chugging fading.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with the red and white rocket-like vehicle, the Ракета-транспорт, crashing through a snowy forest at night with loud engine and crashing sounds, emitting sparks and smoke as it tumbles across the snow-covered ground, causing a small explosion of snow and debris. A Santa-подобный персонаж, dressed in a red robe, stumbles out, brushing snow off his coat with a concerned expression. He is soon joined by a mischievous Бандит в чёрных шляпах, who crouches nearby, laughing and excitedly gesturing, exclaiming in a gruff voice, "Иди отсюда поскорей, с дурацкой птицею, господей!" and threatening, "А ёлку сам тебе срублю и сам зайчатам порублю!". A child-like voice then comments, "Ёлочка, действительно, прекрасная! Прекрасная!", as whimsical, slightly melancholic music begins. The Santa-like figure pulls out a Белый кролик from his sack and hands it to the bandit, who eagerly accepts it. The bandit then produces a crate filled with gold coins, which he places on the snow. A philosophical male voice, likely the Santa-like figure, laments, "Эх ты, сказка-небылица, ни к кому ты не нужна! Новой сказке поклониться, сказка старая должна!", to which another male voice, possibly the bandit, counters, "Так не должен, так не может умный леший говорить! Сказка старая поможет сказку новую творить!". Suddenly, a large black Медведь emerges from the shadows, sniffs the crate, and snatches it with a visual growl, causing the bandit to recoil in surprise. The Santa-like figure, now joined by a young boy in a blue coat, the Мальчик в коричневом пальто и белой шапке, climbs back into the rocket, which is now upright and repaired. The boy grips the steering wheel tightly as the rocket lifts off with a whoosh and soaring engine sounds, accompanied by dramatic, triumphant music. The rocket flies over the snowy landscape with the bandit and the bear chasing after it. The rocket soars into the night sky, leaving a trail of sparks behind, as the bandit and bear continue their pursuit through the forest. The scene ends with the rocket flying high above the snow-covered rooftops of a distant town, the stars shining brightly in the dark sky, as the music fades out.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a wide, sweeping night view of a snow-covered, whimsical town under a starry sky, as a red-and-white rocket-like vehicle, the Ракета-транспорт, crashes through the air with a dramatic orchestral swell and the sound of its impact, trailing smoke and sparks before tumbling into the town. From the wreckage emerges a Santa-подобный персонаж, a tall, elderly man in a red robe, who stumbles out brushing snow off his coat, accompanied by a whimsical, magical orchestral piece. He is soon joined by a flurry of descending objects, including a white rabbit (Белый кролик) and a grey rabbit (Серый кролик) parachuting down with colorful umbrellas, along with various toys and gifts. The Santa-подобный персонаж catches the white rabbit and gestures as if directing the falling items. The scene shifts to a massive, decorated Christmas tree (Рождественская ёлка), adorned with ornaments, beads, and lights, which grows taller as the falling gifts land on its branches, while the music transitions to a festive and grand orchestral piece. The Santa-подобный персонаж climbs the tree, placing the final decorations, and stands proudly at the top as the clock on a nearby icy tower (Изогнутые часы в ледяной стене) strikes midnight with distinct chimes. The tree is now fully lit, glowing with festive lights, and the Santa-подобный персонаж raises his arms in triumph, surrounded by cheering animals (Фестивальные животные) — including the two rabbits, a bear (Медведь), and other woodland creatures — who wave and celebrate. With a deep, booming, joyful voice, the Santa-подобный персонаж declares in Russian, "Эту ёлку и игрушки я из царства сказок вам привёз. С Новым годом, дорогие дети!" (This tree and toys I brought you from the kingdom of fairy tales. Happy New Year, dear children!), to which a chorus of children's voices enthusiastically responds, "С Новым годом!" (Happy New Year!), amplifying the joyful and magical atmosphere of the snow-covered, radiantly illuminated town.
